name: interaction-design
description: Design and implement microinteractions, motion design, transitions, and user feedback patterns. Use when adding polish to UI interactions, implementing loading states, or creating delightful user experiences.
capabilities: Design and implement microinteraction patterns for buttons, forms, and UI controls
Create smooth CSS animations and transitions for state changes
Implement loading states and progress indicators
Build scroll-triggered animations and reveal effects
Apply motion design principles for better user feedback
Select appropriate animation libraries for different use cases
author: wshobson
securitySummary: Static analysis flagged patterns in markdown documentation files. Evaluation confirms all findings are FALSE POSITIVES caused by the analyzer mistaking markdown syntax (backticks for code formatting) and technical terminology for security-sensitive code patterns. This is a pure documentation/reference skill containing markdown files about interaction design patterns with no executable code.
valueStatement: Create engaging user experiences with well-designed microinteractions, animations, and motion patterns. This skill provides comprehensive guidance on implementing smooth transitions, loading states, and user feedback mechanisms.
userTitle: Design polished UI microinteractions and animations
limitations: Does not generate custom animation assets or video content
Does not provide design files (Figma, Sketch, etc.)
Does not integrate with specific animation software tools
Does not cover accessibility guidelines for animations

useCases.title: Adding polish to button interactions
useCases.description: Design subtle hover effects, click feedback, and state transitions for buttons and form controls that make your interface feel responsive and polished.

useCases.title: Creating engaging loading experiences
useCases.description: Replace generic spinners with delightful loading animations, skeleton screens, and progress indicators that keep users informed and engaged.

useCases.targetUser: Creative developers and landing page builders
useCases.title: Implementing scroll-based storytelling
useCases.description: Create scroll-triggered animations that reveal content, guide users through pages, and add narrative motion to long-form content.

promptTemplates.title: Basic animation implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to add a smooth hover animation to my button. The button should have a subtle scale up effect on hover and scale back down on release. Please provide the CSS and any JavaScript needed.

promptTemplates.title: Loading state design
promptTemplates.prompt: My application shows a loading spinner while fetching data. The spinner feels generic and jarring. Help me design a more engaging loading experience using skeleton screens or animated placeholders.
promptTemplates.description: My application shows a loading spinner while fetching data. The spinner feels generic and jarring. Help me design a more engaging loading experience using skeleton screens or animated placeholders.

promptTemplates.title: Scroll animation setup
promptTemplates.prompt: I want elements on my landing page to fade in and slide up as the user scrolls down. Please show me how to implement scroll-triggered animations using intersection observers.

promptTemplates.title: Microinteraction system
promptTemplates.prompt: I'm building a design system and need consistent microinteraction patterns. Help me create a cohesive set of interactions for buttons, inputs, cards, and modals that follow the same motion principles.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a consistent interaction design system

bestPractices: Keep animations short (100-300ms) for optimal perceived responsiveness
Use easing curves (ease-out, cubic-bezier) for natural-feeling motion
Reduce or disable animations for users who prefer reduced motion
antiPatterns: Using animations longer than 500ms for simple state changes
Animating all properties (focus on opacity and transform for performance)
Ignoring accessibility preferences for reduced motion

faq.question: What are the best animation durations for microinteractions?
faq.answer: Microinteractions should feel instant. Use 100-200ms for simple state changes like button hovers, and 200-400ms for more complex transitions like modal reveals.

faq.question: How do I ensure animations are accessible?
faq.answer: Use the prefers-reduced-motion media query to detect user preferences. Provide alternatives or disable animations for users who request reduced motion.

faq.question: Should I use CSS or JavaScript for animations?
faq.answer: Use CSS for simple transitions and keyframe animations. Use JavaScript (requestAnimationFrame, Intersection Observer) for complex, interactive, or scroll-triggered animations.

faq.question: How do I create smooth 60fps animations?
faq.answer: Animate only transform and opacity properties. Avoid animating layout properties like width, height, or margin which trigger reflows. Use will-change sparingly.

faq.question: What easing functions feel most natural?
faq.answer: Use ease-out for elements entering the viewport and ease-in-out for elements moving within the viewport. Custom cubic-bezier curves offer more natural feel than standard keywords.

faq.question: How do I choose between CSS animations and animation libraries?
faq.answer: Use native CSS for simple, performant animations. Consider libraries like Framer Motion, GSAP, or anime.js when you need complex timelines, physics-based animations, or advanced sequencing.
